description:
**Milestone:** Settings Persistence

Add SchedulerSettings interface to libs/types/src/global-settings.ts with taskOverrides: Record<string, { enabled?: boolean; cronExpression?: string }> and add DEFAULT_SCHEDULER_SETTINGS constant included in DEFAULT_GLOBAL_SETTINGS. Update SchedulerService.start() to read taskOverrides from settingsService and apply them after registering tasks. Update enableTask(), disableTask(), and updateTaskSchedule() to write back to GlobalSettings via settingsService when changing a built-in task. Pass settingsService into SchedulerService via a new setSettingsService() method or by extending the existing initialize() signature. Update scheduler.module.ts to wire settingsService into SchedulerService after initialization.

**Files to Modify:**
- libs/types/src/global-settings.ts
- apps/server/src/services/scheduler-service.ts
- apps/server/src/services/scheduler.module.ts

**Acceptance Criteria:**
- [ ] SchedulerSettings interface exported from @protolabsai/types
- [ ] DEFAULT_SCHEDULER_SETTINGS included in DEFAULT_GLOBAL_SETTINGS
- [ ] Disabling a built-in task writes to data/settings.json under schedulerSettings.taskOverrides
- [ ] Server restart restores disabled state — task remains disabled
- [ ] Cron expression override also persists and restores across restart
- [ ] npm run build:packages and full typecheck pass

**Complexity:** medium

**Guardrails:** If this phase involves new or changed behavior, include tests that verify correctness. If this phase adds, removes, or changes a user-facing feature, API endpoint, configuration option, or service, update the relevant documentation in `docs/` to reflect those changes.
title: SchedulerSettings Type and Persistence Wiring
